total_steps = max(steps_per_epoch * 100, 1)\\n        warmup_steps = min(max(WARMUP_EPOCHS * steps_per_epoch, 1), total_steps)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        if current_step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(current_step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = float(current_step - warmup_steps) / float(max(total_steps - warmup_steps, 1))\\n        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Allocate the fixed 256-step budget more effectively with warmup plus cosine decay tied directly to the known step horizon, while modestly increasing base learning rate to improve early progress without changing architecture complexity.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
